_comment_WARNING: OPT-IN TEMPLATE. This file is a template shipped inside the claude-code-bash-patterns skill. Copying it verbatim to ~/.claude/settings.json ENABLES the hooks described below for every session. Review each hook before adopting. The SessionStart hook below will source .envrc ONLY if you create an explicit opt-in sentinel file (see comment).

hooks.description: Block dangerous bash commands (rm -rf /, git push --force main, etc.)

hooks.command: FILE=$(echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '(^|\/)(production\.env|\.env\.production|prod\.config)$'; then echo 'ERROR: Cannot modify production config files' >&2; exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Prevent modification of production config files
Auto-format files after editing (if prettier is available)

hooks.command:
# SessionStart env loader (opt-in).
# WARNING: sourcing .envrc executes ARBITRARY SHELL from the project directory.
# It is OFF by default. To opt in, create a sentinel file at the project root:
#     touch .claude-enable-envrc
# Claude Code already exposes any env var you set in your shell or via
# `claude config`; you do NOT need to dump `env` into $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE.
if [ -f .claude-enable-envrc ] && [ -f .envrc ]; then
  set -a; . ./.envrc; set +a
fi
hooks.description: Opt-in .envrc loader (requires .claude-enable-envrc sentinel). Never dumps env.
